heading: Procedure
body:
- Click the Snapshots sub-tab.

- In the Actions column, expand the
   Actions menu, and click
  Download for the snapshot that you want to download.

  Atlas generates a one-time use download link that expires
  within 1 hour after its creation. For AWS (Amazon Web Services) or Azure (Microsoft Azure),
  Atlas also generates a one-time-use download link for any
  [set up a private endpoint for a cluster](cluster-private-endpoint) in the same region as the
  snapshot.

  The amount of time to create this link increases with the size of
  the Atlas cluster.

Once the download is ready, Atlas:

- Emails you an alert that your snapshot download is ready.

- Displays the download link in the Restores & Downloads
  tab.

> **Note:** 
>
> Available via API
>
> As another option, you can request a restore snapshot using the
> [API](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroupclusterbackuprestorejob). The
> Atlas Administration API returns the download link in the
> `deliveryUrl` field for downloading over the public internet
> and in the `privateDownloadDeliveryUrls` field for downloading
> over the private endpoint in the same region as the
> snapshot.

If the current project IP access list ranges do not cover the
target client IP (Internet Protocol) or CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) address, click Add or
Modify your IP Addresses to make changes to your Atlas
project IP access list.

- Click the Restores & Downloads sub-tab.

- Navigate to the restore snapshot you created.

- Click Download.

Atlas compresses the snapshot into a `.tar.gz` file. This
archive includes the snapshot and the mongod logs.

- Extract the files in the archive.

  > **Example:** The following command uses the `tar` utility to extract a `tar``archive with ``gzip` compression.
  >
  > ```shell
  > tar -xvzf ~/Downloads/mongodb-snapshots/my-cluster-snapshot.tar.gz
  > ```

- Access the data files by starting a [mongod](manual/reference/program/mongod) instance
  on the host and pointing it at the extract directory using the
  [--dbpath](manual/reference/program/mongod) option. To learn more, see
  [Start mongod Processes](manual/tutorial/manage-mongodb-processes).

  > **Example:** The following command starts a [mongod](manual/reference/program/mongod) instance using
  > the extracted data file directory:
  >
  > ```shell
  > mongod --dbpath ~/Downloads/mongodb-snapshots/my-cluster-snapshot/
  > ```
